title: Glare sensitivity device and methods of use thereof
abstract: A device to aid in the measurement of glare experienced by an individual, and a method and use of such device, are provided. The device includes a head mount adapted to be releasably mountable on the head of the individual, and one or more light sources secured to the head mount. The one or more light sources are positioned such that, when the head mount is operatively positioned on the head, the light sources shine simultaneously into both eyes, the light being adapted to elicit a predetermined veiling luminance (disability glare veiling luminance) based on the age of the individual.
claims:
Claims Claims: 1. A device to aid in the measurement of glare experienced by an individual, the device comprising: a head mount adapted to be releasably mountable on the head of the individual; and one or more light sources secured to the head mount, the one or more light sources positioned such that, when the head mount is operatively positioned on the head, the light sources shine simultaneously into both eyes, the light being adapted to elicit a predetermined veiling luminance based on the age of the individual. 2. The device of claim 1, wherein the one or more light sources is an LED strip. 3. The device of claim 2, wherein the predetermined veiling luminance elicited depends on the age of the individual: 4. The device of claim 3, wherein the one or more light sources is positioned on the head mount to shine light simultaneously into both eyes from above. 5. The device of claim 4, wherein the head mount comprises a visor and the one or more light sources is secured to an underside of the visor, allowing consistent 

positioning of lights relative to the eyes, allowing the one or more light sources to remain in position despite head or body movement. The device of claim 5, wherein the head mount comprises a bar secured transversely to the visor parallel to the LED strip, the bar extending generally perpendicular from the visor to direct light from the LED strip towards both eyes of the individual. The device of claim 6, wherein the visor is adjustable relative to head mount, allowing the strip of LED lights to be adjusted relative to both eyes of the individual. The device of claim 7, further comprising a variable resistor operatively coupled to the one or more light sources. The device of claim 8, wherein the variable resistor is a rheostat. A method to aid in the measuring of glare experienced by an individual, the method comprising: taking a first baseline visual measurement; shining light into both eyes of the individual simultaneously using the device of claim 1 to elicit a predetermined veiling luminance based on the age of the individual; taking a second visual measurement; and calculating a glare measurement from the first and second visual measurements. 

The method of claim 10, wherein the method further comprises, prior to the shining step, releasably mounting the device of claim 1 to the individual's head, with the one or more light sources positioned proximate both eyes of the individual. The method of claim 11, wherein the predetermined veiling luminance elicited depends on the age of the individual: The method of claim 11, wherein the shining comprises shining light into both eyes from above. The method of claim 13, wherein the method further comprises reducing external light sources when shining light into both eyes of the individual simultaneously. The method of claim 11, wherein the head mount of the device comprises a visor and the one or more light sources is secured to an underside of the visor, the method further comprising adjusting the visor to adjust the angle at which one or more light sources is positioned relative to both eyes of the individual. The method of claim 15, further comprising adjusting the intensity of light shining into both eyes of the individual simultaneously. 

The method of claim 11, wherein light is shined into both eyes of the individual simultaneously for a specified period of time; and the method further comprises removing the light source and waiting another specified period of time prior to taking the second visual measurement. Use of the device of claim 1 to aid in the measuring of glare experienced by an individual. The use of claim 18 for taking a glare sensitivity measurement. The use of claim 18 for taking a glare recovery measurement. 22
